---
title: "Interest Rate, Bond Duration, and Bond Market Sensitivity"
author: 
  - Lisa Liang (yl783)
  - Siwei Chen (siweichen)
format: pdf
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Introduction

Monetary policy changes, particularly adjustments to the **Federal Funds Rate**, influence financial markets through multiple channels. One of the most direct channels is the **bond market**, where bond prices respond to changes in interest rates.

This project examines:

> **How do changes in the Federal Funds Rate influence bond returns across bonds, Treasury securities, and TIPS?**

The analysis focuses on how **bond duration and maturity amplify interest rate risk**, and identifies which segments of the bond market are most sensitive to monetary tightening.

Understanding these relationships is important for **public policy and financial stability**, since large rate changes can create significant valuation shifts across financial institutions holding fixed-income assets.

---

# Data

This project combines **three datasets**.

## 1. WRDS Bond Returns Dataset

Source: WRDS Bond Returns (Beta)

This dataset contains detailed bond-level information including:

- bond prices
- yields
- duration
- maturity
- credit ratings
- returns

The dataset is highly disaggregated and contains **individual security-level observations**.

---

## 2. Interest Rate Dataset

Source: Federal Reserve / WRDS rates monthly dataset.

Key variables include:

- Federal Funds Rate
- Treasury yield curve measures
- monthly interest rate changes

These variables allow us to measure **monetary policy shocks**.

---

## 3. Treasury / TIPS Dataset

Source: CRSP Treasury Securities Database.

Variables include:

- Treasury yields
- duration
- maturity
- bond returns

This dataset allows comparison between **nominal bonds and inflation-protected securities (TIPS)**.

---

# Load Packages

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import altair as alt

bonds = pd.read_csv("data/wrds_bond_returns.csv")
rates = pd.read_csv("data/rates_monthly.csv")
tips = pd.read_csv("data/tips.csv")

bonds.columns = [c.lower() for c in bonds.columns]
rates.columns = [c.lower() for c in rates.columns]
tips.columns = [c.lower() for c in tips.columns]

bonds["month"] = pd.to_datetime(bonds["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
rates["month"] = pd.to_datetime(rates["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
tips["month"] = pd.to_datetime(tips["tdatdt"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

rates = rates.sort_values("month")
rates["d_ffr"] = rates["fedfunds"].diff()

rates_core = rates[["month","fedfunds","d_ffr"]]

bonds_core = bonds[[
    "month",
    "issue_id",
    "bond_type",
    "duration",
    "tmt",
    "yield",
    "price_eom",
    "ret_eom"
]].copy()

df = bonds_core.merge(rates_core, on="month", how="left")

tips_std = pd.DataFrame({
    "month": tips["month"],
    "bond_type": "TIPS",
    "duration": tips["tmduratn"],
    "yield": tips["tmyld"],
    "ret_eom": tips["tmretnua"]
})

tips_std = tips_std.merge(rates_core, on="month", how="left")

df = pd.concat([df, tips_std], ignore_index=True)

df["dur_bucket"] = pd.cut(
    df["duration"],
    bins=[-np.inf,2,5,8,12,np.inf],
    labels=["<=2","2-5","5-8","8-12","12+"]
)

alt.Chart(df.sample(10000)).mark_circle(opacity=0.3).encode(
    x="duration",
    y="ret_eom",
    color="bond_type"
).properties(
    title="Bond Duration vs Monthly Returns"
)

ts = (
    df.dropna(subset=["dur_bucket"])
      .groupby(["month","dur_bucket"])
      .ret_eom.mean()
      .reset_index()
)

alt.Chart(ts).mark_line().encode(
    x="month:T",
    y="ret_eom",
    color="dur_bucket"
).properties(
    title="Average Bond Returns by Duration Bucket"
)

heat = (
    df[df["d_ffr"]>0]
      .groupby(["dur_bucket","bond_type"])
      .ret_eom.mean()
      .reset_index()
)

alt.Chart(heat).mark_rect().encode(
    x="dur_bucket",
    y="bond_type",
    color="ret_eom"
).properties(
    title="Bond Returns During Tightening Periods"
)

bubble = (
    df.dropna(subset=["d_ffr"])
      .groupby(["month","bond_type"])
      .agg(avg_ret=("ret_eom","mean"),
           d_ffr=("d_ffr","mean"))
      .reset_index()
)

alt.Chart(bubble).mark_circle(size=80).encode(
    x="d_ffr",
    y="avg_ret",
    color="bond_type"
).properties(
    title="Interest Rate Changes vs Bond Returns"
)
